# Installation

Installing RiverDrop is straightforward and adheres to standard macOS app procedures.

## Download and Install

1.  **Download**: Obtain the latest RiverDrop DMG from the official website or your purchase confirmation email.
2.  **Mount**: Double-click the DMG file to mount it.
3.  **Install**: Drag the RiverDrop icon into your **Applications** folder.

![Install RiverDrop](images/install-dmg.png){fig-alt="Dragging RiverDrop into the Applications folder"}

## First Launch and Security

When you first open RiverDrop, macOS may display a Gatekeeper warning because the app was downloaded from the internet.

1.  Locate RiverDrop in your Applications folder.
2.  **Right-click** (or Control-click) the app and select **Open**.
3.  In the dialog that appears, click **Open** again to confirm.

::: {.callout-note}
RiverDrop is notarized by Apple to ensure it is free from known malware and safe to run on your Mac.
:::

## Setting Up Command-Line Tools

For advanced features like accelerated transfers and deep content search, RiverDrop relies on standard open-source tools:

### Rsync
Used for high-speed transfers and synchronization. While macOS includes a version of `rsync`, it is significantly outdated. We strongly recommend installing a modern version (3.0+) for better performance.

```bash
brew install rsync
```

### Ripgrep (rg)
Used for extremely fast local and remote content searching.

```bash
brew install ripgrep
```

## Granting Permissions

The first time you browse a local folder (like your Desktop or Documents), macOS will ask for permission to access that directory. Click **Allow** or **Open** to grant RiverDrop access to manage your files.

![macOS Folder Access Prompt](images/permission-prompt.png){fig-alt="macOS asking for folder access permissions"}
